summary: Project a premium, sober B2B environment through neutral surfaces and restrained brand accents.
Prioritize operational clarity and transactional trust over decorative aesthetics.
Ensure high data readability for complex marketplace and dashboard workflows.

forbidden_brand_color_uses: Success, error, or warning status indicators
semantic_override_rules: Semantic tokens always take precedence over brand purple for transactional feedback.
Never mix brand purple with semantic colors on a single component surface.

priority_rules: Critical errors must use semantic error red (#BA1A1A).
Verification and trust states must use semantic verified indigo (#1A237E).
Awaiting states must use semantic pending cyan (#006064).
status_mapping_rules: Green is reserved for financial completion (Paid) and generic Success.
Teal is reserved for temporal occupation (Booked).
Orange is reserved for capacity limits (Full) and generic Warnings.

surface_layering: Maximum 3 layers of visual depth (Background > Surface > Card/Sheet).
Use Outline Variant (#CCC4CE) for secondary separation instead of shadow.
background_rules: Never apply brand colors to the root scaffold background.
gradient_rules: Gradients are strictly forbidden across all UI surfaces.
All fills must be solid and deterministic.
outline_rules: All input fields and secondary cards must use a 1dp outline.
Focus states must use a 2dp primary outline.

when_to_use: High-priority marketplace cards to provide tactile depth.

titles: Headline Large for root screen titles only.
Title Large for top app bar titles.
body: Body Large for user-generated content and descriptions.
Body Medium for system labels and input text.
metadata: Label Small or Body Small for timestamps, counts, and fine print.
metrics: Display Small for primary KPIs on dashboards using tabular numbers.

shape_consistency: Buttons and inputs must share corner_sm (8dp).
Modal sheets must use corner_xl (28dp) for top edges.
spacing_consistency: 8dp grid (xs=8, md=16, lg=24) must govern all padding and margins.
16dp (md) is the mandatory screen edge margin.

stacking: Cards may not be nested within other cards.
density: Use 16dp internal padding for standard cards.
Use 12dp internal padding for dense dashboard widgets.

readability: Labels must be persistent and placed above the field.
Input text must use body_large for maximum clarity.
validation_visibility: Errors must be displayed immediately below the field in semantic red.
Validation icons must accompany all error messages.

booking_rules: Booking lifecycle states (Booked, Pending, Cancelled) must appear as top-anchored banners on detail screens.
Use specific icons (Calendar, Clock, X) to distinguish temporal states.
payment_rules: Amounts must be displayed in bold tabular figures.
Payment status (Paid, Unpaid, Refunded) must use high-contrast emerald, berry, or lavender containers.

chat_rules: Unread messages must be marked with a 8dp brand purple dot.

metric_rules: Primary metrics must be 1.5x larger than secondary labels.
Trend indicators (arrows) must use semantic success or error colors.
data_density_rules: Prioritize tabular layout over card layout for management screens with > 10 items.

contrast: All text must meet WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1) against its immediate container.
Branded elements must ensure white on purple contrast exceeds 4.5:1.
status_signaling: Color must never be the sole indicator of status (always include icons).
Focus rings must be high-visibility 2dp primary color.
touch_targets: Interactive elements must maintain a minimum 48dp x 48dp target area.

dos: Use outlines for grouping data-heavy content.
Apply tabular lining to all financial figures.
Include iconography in all status indicators.
donts: Do not use brand purple for backgrounds.
Do not use fluid or organic shapes.
Do not use 3D effects, gloss, or shadows on buttons.
Do not use more than two font weights on a single card.
anti_patterns: Using red for cancellations that aren't technical failures.
Omitting labels from icons in navigation or critical actions.
Using elevation to separate every item in a list.
